title: Elaphoglossum lanceolatum
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum lanceolatum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification suggests that it is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes many other types of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Elaphoglossum itself is also a large and widespread group, with over 800 species found in tropical and subtropical areas. Elaphoglossum lanceolatum's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. intro: Elaphoglossum lanceolatum is a species of epiphytic fern, specifically a member of the family Dryopteridaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum lanceolatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum lanceolatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams that are used to treat various health conditions.
